title: Unidentified Acme Droid Service salesman
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{GAnom}} {{Character |type=Corporate |image=[[File:Acme_Droid_Service_salesman.png]] |name=Unidentified Acme Droid Service salesman |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Alien/Legends|Alien]][[Droids (1986) 7|''Droids'' (1986) 7]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns= |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Green]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Green |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Acme Droid Service]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Next! Next! Come on, keep it moving!|The alien doing their job|}} An [[Alien/Legends|alien]] worked as a '''salesman''' for [[Acme Droid Service]] in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] pair [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] took refuge from [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] within the service building and attempted to blend in with other droids using the parts available for sale. After the [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] left, the pair tried to leave without paying for the parts. The salesman halted the droids and threatened C-3PO as all sales were final. Before the alien could harm C-3PO, R2-D2 shot a [[Laser/Legends|laser]] into the salesman's rear end, causing them to release the droid and run out of the building. ==Biography== {{Quote|Let's get out of here!''"
"''Not without providing your master charge disk you don't!|C-3PO being stopped by the salesman|}} In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],The events of [[Droids (1986) 7|''Droids'' (1986) 7]] occur before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] simultaneously with {{Film|IV}}, which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] worked as a [[Merchant/Legends|saleman]] for [[Acme Droid Service]] in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. They stood behind a desk and took orders from several [[Droid/Legends|droids]] when the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] took refuge in the building from [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. The fugitive droids got in line behind multiple other droid customers and were eventually called to the front by the alien. C-3PO ordered multiple parts to help the pair blend in with the other droids until the stormtroopers had passed. [[File:AggressiveSales-Droids7.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The salesman threatens C-3PO]] With the coast clear, the two droids threw off their disguises and attempted to leave Acme Droid Service without paying. The alien left the booth and stood in the droids' path carrying a [[Mace (weapon)/Legends|spiked mace]], telling the droids that they were not permitted to leave before providing their [[master charge disk]]s. C-3PO claimed that they were simply looking and was then picked up by the salesman, who fiercely exclaimed that all sales were final as they smashed their [[Melee weapon/Legends|mace]] into a wall. They raised their [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]] at the protocol droid before his astromech companion shot a [[Laser/Legends|laser]] into the salesman's rear end, setting it on [[Fire/Legends|fire]]. The alien released the droid and leapt into the air yelping before running out of the building into a pair of stormtroopers, who then pursued the droids. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|B-but we were just looking and-GLK.''"
"''All purchases are final!|C-3PO and the salesman|}} The salesman was a large [[Color/Legends|green]]-skinned [[Gender/Legends|male]] alien with green [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. They ran Acme Droid Service aggressively, threatening droids who attempted to leave without paying, and wasn't fond of individuals simply looking, derogatorily referring to them as "lookers." ==Equipment== {{Quote|I'll show you what we do to lookers around... h--YOW YOW!|The salesman threatening C-3PO before getting set on fire|}} They wore a red full-body [[Clothing/Legends|suit]] with blue accents on the [[Limb/Legends|arms]], legs, neck ring, and [[Boot/Legends|boots]]. The shoulders of the suit had circular blue symbols with a yellow [[Star/Legends|star]] in the center. Around their waist they wore a blue belt with multiple pockets and a tool holder for a pair of [[pliers]]. The salesman wielded a spiked mace they used to threaten non-paying customers. ==Behind the scenes== The Acme Droid Service salesman appeared in the [[Droids (1986) 7|seventh]] issue of the [[1986]] [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series. The issue was written by [[David Manak]], penciled by [[Ernie Colón]], and published by [[Marvel Comics]] on [[January 13]], [[1987]].''[[Marvel Age]]'' 48 ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of unidentified Acme Droid Service salesman}} *[[Droids (1986) 7|''Droids'' (1986) 7]] {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Businesspeople]] [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Individuals with unspecified pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Tatooinians]]
